homepage.text_excerpt: Alex Davies-Jones for Pontypridd Skip to main content About | Amdanaf i News | Newyddion CAMPAIGNS | ymgyrchoedd SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts Cost of Living Brochure (English) Cost of Living Brochure (Welsh) Save our A&E All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling Pontypridd & Taff Ely Flood Report 2020 More Menu Alex Davies-Jones Labour MP for Pontypridd Alex Davies-Jones MP on a bridge in Pontypridd About Alex Alex Davies-Jones has proudly been the Labour Member of Parliament for Pontypridd since 2019, and now serves in Government as the Minister for Victims and Violence Against Women and Girls. In her previous role as a Shadow Minister, Alex led Labour's legislative scrutiny of the Online Safety Act in the House of Commons. Alex worked with MPs across the House on issues such as online violence against women and girls (VAWG), in a cross-party effort to strengthen the legislation. If you require assistance from Alex you can email the team at [email protected] Other organisations that may be able to help include: Citizens Advice – Provide free, confidential information and advice to assist people with money, legal, consumer, and other problems. Website: www.citizensadvice.org.uk/wales Telephone: 0800 702 2020 Rhondda Cynon Taf Council – For local emergencies: Telephone: 01443 425011 for emergency information Home Office – For issues regarding visas or immigration Telephone: 0300 790 6268 Department for Work & Pensions – For issues relating to welfare, Universal Credit, and Job Seekers’ Allowance Website: www.gov.uk/guidance/contact-the-department-for-work-and-pensions-about-its-policies Welsh Water – For issues related to emergency flooding or repair work Telephone: 0800 281432 National Grid – Powercuts Telephone: 0800 678 3105 NHS – Latest health advice across Wales Website: www.wales.nhs.uk Telephone: For non-emergencies ring 111. In serious circumstances ring 999. Local foodbank information Website: https://www.pontypridd.foodbank.org.uk">www.pontypridd.foodbank.org.uk Email: [email protected] Telephone: 07562 137392 South Wales Police – Report a crime online Website: www.south-wales.police.uk/ro/report/ MIND – Mental Health Support Website: www.mind.org.uk/information-support/helplines Telephone: 0300 123 3393 Domestic Abuse Support – National Helpline Website: www.nationaldahelpline.org.uk Telephone: 0808 2000247 Read my bio Follow on social media Contact details 10 Market Street Pontypridd CF37 2ST 10 Market Street Pontypridd CF37 2ST '> View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by and on behalf of Alex Davies-Jones at Welsh Labour, 1 Cathedral Road, Cardiff, CF11 9HA. pages.title: About | Amdanaf i - Alex Davies-Jones for Pontypridd
pages.text_excerpt: About | Amdanaf i - Alex Davies-Jones for Pontypridd Skip to main content About | Amdanaf i News | Newyddion CAMPAIGNS | ymgyrchoedd SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts Cost of Living Brochure (English) Cost of Living Brochure (Welsh) Save our A&E All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling Pontypridd & Taff Ely Flood Report 2020 More Menu About | Amdanaf i Hi there! My name is Alex, and I’m your Welsh Labour Member of Parliament for Pontypridd. I have been the MP since 2019 and in my role as your elected representative, I am able to assist you with any queries you may have ranging from support with welfare to housing, and I can also escalate enquiries to Ministers in Westminster on your behalf. I am Pontypridd born and bred and was born in Church Village – the Welsh Valleys really do run through my blood. I spent my early years training to be an Air Cadet in Pontyclun, sang in the National Youth Choir for Wales and spent my teen years socialising with friends at Ynysyngharad Park. I joined my local community council seven years ago because I wanted to get more involved with the local area that I know and love. From 2017-2022 I became a Local Authority Councillor, representing the area that I live in, Tonyrefail. I was first elected to represent Pontypridd in Parliament in December 2019 and am a proud and active member of Unite and the Musician’s Union. Outside of politics I enjoy singing, sport – with rugby and wrestling being two personal favourites – and catching up with friends. At my 2019 campaign launch with local Labour MS, Mick Antoniw One of my biggest passions in life (alongside family and politics of course) is my love for shopping, watching sport (being Welsh, rugby is a big one in my household) and live music. Most recently I’ve been appointed as a Patron of the Tonyrefail Ladies’ Choir – a choir which I have fond memories of singing in myself! I use my time as your Member of Parliament to focus on a range of issues that affect us locally — I have used my voice loudly and proudly to stand up for the issues that you care about most and would continue to do so if re-elected. In recent years I’ve campaigned for better flood protections for the Valleys after the devastating 2020 floods across Pontypridd and RCT, and I also campaigned successfully to prevent closure of A&E services at the Royal Glamorgan Hospital. pages.title: SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts - Alex Davies-Jones for Pontypridd
pages.h1: SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts
pages.text_excerpt: SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts - Alex Davies-Jones for Pontypridd Skip to main content About | Amdanaf i News | Newyddion CAMPAIGNS | ymgyrchoedd SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts Cost of Living Brochure (English) Cost of Living Brochure (Welsh) Save our A&E All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling Pontypridd & Taff Ely Flood Report 2020 More Menu 31st August, 2023 SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts A car exhaust. Image by Nerivill via pixabay.com. I want to hear from residents about the impact that noisy car exhausts are having on local communities. Vehicles that may have been modified may make ‘gunshot’ noises which have previously impacted specific parts of our community, including in Talbot Green and Llantrisant, but I’m concerned by reports of this anti-social behaviour taking place in other areas too. Exhausts specifically designed to backfire in this way are illegal – the sound emitted by an exhaust fitted to a standard car must not exceed 80dB. If you believe your local area has been affected by individuals using modified exhausts, please do fill in the short survey HERE . With your help, I will be able to continue to campaign in Westminster for more to be done to clamp down on this anti-social behaviour. The survey will run for six weeks until 16th October. In accordance with data protection laws, any information you submit will be held securely and not used for any other purpose without your consent or passed on to any third party. Loading… Contact details 10 Market Street Pontypridd CF37 2ST 10 Market Street Pontypridd CF37 2ST '> View on Google Maps Email: [email protected] Further details Promoted by and on behalf of Alex Davies-Jones at Welsh Labour, 1 Cathedral Road, Cardiff, CF11 9HA. pages.title: All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling - Alex Davies-Jones for Pontypridd
pages.text_excerpt: All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling - Alex Davies-Jones for Pontypridd Skip to main content About | Amdanaf i News | Newyddion CAMPAIGNS | ymgyrchoedd SURVEY: Antisocial Behaviour: Loud vehicle exhausts Cost of Living Brochure (English) Cost of Living Brochure (Welsh) Save our A&E All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling Pontypridd & Taff Ely Flood Report 2020 More Menu All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling Front page of All-Party Parliamentary Group inquiry into professional wrestling THURSDAY 8TH APRIL 2021 Today I am thrilled to announce that the All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling is launching our report which has been six months in the making. The cross-party inquiry panel, led by All-Party Parliamentary Group on Wrestling co-chairs Alex Davies-Jones MP and Mark Fletcher MP has taken written and oral evidence on the professional wrestling industry from some 80 wrestlers, promoters, ring crew, training school operators, academics, health care professionals and others connected to the British wrestling world. The report marks the first time that this unregulated industry which crosses the threshold between sport and theatre has been analysed in a systemic way by parliamentarians, and the first official intervention in nearly 80 years. Main recommendations Wrestling has fallen between the categories of sport and theatre, sometimes characterised as sports entertainment. The panel recommends that wrestling training schools be considered as ‘sporting’ and shows, promotions and associated activity be considered ‘theatrical’ or ‘artistic’ or ‘performative’, in respect of future guidance, legislation and regulation. The minimum health and safety standards across independent wrestling in Britain is worryingly low. The parliamentarians have recommended a raft of measures most notably, enhanced requirements under the licencing and insurance arrangements wrestling companies have in place, and the adoption of concussion protocols. Signed PAR Q fitness forms, guidance from the Health and Safety Executive and minimum first aid training are also recommended. Wrestling’s own MeToo movement, known as SpeakingOut highlighted stories of sexual abuse, assault and predation. The report makes a number of recommendations in response to the movement, including hardwiring the voice of abuse survivors into British wrestling, the development of practical guides for first responders and working to establish case management systems to address allegations of abuse. Wrestling training schools have insufficient protections in place, particularly given they are regularly working with minors. The report recommends that, as is proposed under current legislation, sports coaches be recognised as a position of trust for the purposes of child sexual offences and that wrestling coaches be considered sports coaches for this purpose. A number of interim steps in relation to safeguarding and data protection are also proposed. The wrestling industry suffers from a lack of official representation, and the report recommends the establishment of trade bodies to more effectively promote British wrestling. Other recommendations include a call for increased data collection about the industry, appropriate memorialisation of great British wrestling talents building on the Professional Wrestling Hall of Fame for Scotland, work to better understand the gender pay gap in wrestling, infrastructure to support wrestlers working overseas and better measures to accommodate visiting talent, and creating a safety standard for wrestling rings. In Parliament I chair the group along with Mark Fletcher MP and our comments are below: “We are delighted to take what we hope will be a first step towards strengthening and improving the British wrestling industry which brings joy to so many people, ourselves included. We don’t do reports that sit on a shelf and look forward putting our recommendations into action in the coming weeks.” Sports Minister Nigel Huddleston said:
